decks.notes: The focus of this premodern combo deck is to quickly and efficiently get Phyrexian Dreadnought onto the battlefield with the help of Stifle and Vision Charm to create a powerful threat. It relies on a mix of cantrips and counterspells to control the game and protect the combo piece, while also having disruption tools like Foil and Counterspell to thwart opposing strategies. The deck aims to win the game through a strong synergy between its key cards and leverage its combo potential to secure victory.

This deck utilizes a combination of enchantresses and enchantments to quickly generate card advantage and mana ramp. By playing and drawing enchantments, the deck can power out powerful spells like Parallax Wave and Opalescence to control the board and win the game. With cards like Mirri's Guile and Sterling Grove, the deck can consistently find the necessary pieces to execute its combo. The goal is to create a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to respond.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game through counter spells and removal spells, while using cards like Standstill to generate card advantage. The deck aims to stall the game with land destruction and control spells until it can win the game with a threat like Exalted Angel or Mishra's Factory. The deck also utilizes card draw and selection spells like Fact or Fiction and Impulse to consistently find the right answers. The goal is to outlast the opponent and win through attrition.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. Using efficient creatures like Wild Mongrel and Basking Rootwalla, along with powerful spells like Lightning Bolt and Grim Lavamancer, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early and often. Survival of the Fittest helps to find key creatures, while cards like Call of the Herd and Arrogant Wurm provide additional threats. With a mix of creatures and burn spells, this deck looks to win the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize.

The main concept of this Goblins aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of fast, cheap creatures like Mogg Fanatic, Goblin Lackey, and Goblin Piledriver. Using cards like Goblin Ringleader and Goblin Matron to find more Goblins, the deck aims to maintain pressure and tempo. The inclusion of cards like Siege-Gang Commander and Goblin Sharpshooter provides additional threats and removal options. With explosive starts and a focus on aggression, this deck looks to quickly establish a dominant board presence and close out games before the opponent can stabilize.

This Goblins deck is focused on playing aggressive creatures quickly and overwhelming the opponent with a swarm of Goblins. The key strategy is to use cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Ringleader to put more Goblins onto the battlefield, while using Goblin Warchief to reduce their cost. Goblin Piledriver can deal massive damage, especially when paired with Goblin Sharpshooter and Siege-Gang Commander. The deck also includes disruption with cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port, along with some removal options like Gempalm Incinerator and Naturalize to help maintain board control.

The deck focuses on utilizing the synergy between cards like Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to pull off a powerful combo that can quickly overwhelm the opponent. By combining powerful counterspells and card draw with the ability to control the tempo of the game, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while setting up its win condition efficiently. The key strategy is to carefully manage resources and timing to set up the combo while maintaining control over the game.

The deck aims to quickly assemble the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a devastating threat with minimal cost. By efficiently managing resources and disrupting the opponent's game plan, the deck seeks to control the pace of the game and secure victory through a decisive and unexpected play. Through a combination of card draw, counterspells, and selective removal, the deck aims to protect its combo pieces and ensure a swift and decisive victory.

The main focus of this deck is fast and aggressive play, utilizing low-cost creatures and disruption spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes efficient removal spells like Smother and Diabolic Edict, along with hand disruption cards like Duress and Cabal Therapy to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The creatures in the deck, such as Hypnotic Specter and Graveborn Muse, are designed to apply pressure on the opponent while also providing card advantage. With a solid mana base and strong synergy between the cards, this mono black aggro deck aims to win the game quickly and decisively.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with enchantments using cards like Attunement and Frantic Search, then using Replenish to bring them all back into play at once. The win condition is to create a massive board presence by turning enchantments like Parallax Wave and Opalescence into powerful creatures. Decree of Silence and Seal of Cleansing provide disruption and control elements, while Mana Leak and Tsabo's Web help to stall the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a powerful combo before they can recover.

The main focus of this deck is to use enchantress cards to draw multiple cards each turn and generate a substantial amount of mana. By playing enchantments that provide card draw and mana ramp, along with cards like Opalescence to turn enchantments into creatures, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful enchantments on the battlefield. The deck also includes combo pieces like Parallax Wave and Replenish to control the board and quickly replenish enchantments that have been removed. The goal is to quickly assemble a dominating board presence and win the game through creature damage or alternate win conditions.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful threat that can be difficult for opponents to deal with. The deck is designed to control the game with counterspells and card draw while setting up the combo as quickly as possible. By efficiently managing resources and disrupting the opponent's strategy, this deck aims to outpace and outmaneuver the opposition to secure a victory.

The main focus of this Mono Black Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and efficient creatures and disruption spells. By utilizing cards like Dark Ritual, Withered Wretch, and Hypnotic Specter, the deck aims to control the board and apply pressure early in the game. With a mix of aggressive creatures like Knight of Stromgald and Nantuko Shade, along with removal spells like Smother and Diabolic Edict, this deck looks to maintain tempo and outpace the opponent to secure a quick victory.

The main focus of this Mono Black Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and disrupt their hand with discard spells. The deck aims to apply pressure early in the game with efficient creatures like Hypnotic Specter and Withered Wretch while also controlling the board with removal spells like Smother and Diabolic Edict. Cabal Therapy and Duress help strip the opponent's hand of key cards, allowing for a smooth path to victory. Mishra's Factory and Nantuko Shade provide additional threats, while Cursed Scroll and Graveborn Muse offer late-game advantages and card advantage.

The deck focuses on utilizing the graveyard as a resource to bring back enchantments like Opalescence and Decree of Silence with Replenish. This combo allows for powerful board control and disruption when combined with cards like Parallax Wave and Seal of Cleansing. The deck aims to quickly set up its enchantment combo while controlling the opponent's threats with removal spells like Swords to Plowshares. The deck's mana base includes Ancient Tomb and fetch lands to consistently cast its spells and execute its game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by denying the opponent resources and maintaining board presence through cards like Standstill and Mishra's Factory. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with counterspells like Mana Leak and Counterspell, while also having removal options like Swords to Plowshares and Wrath of God. The deck also has card draw and selection tools like Fact or Fiction and Impulse to help find key cards. Overall, the deck focuses on grinding out the opponent and outlasting them in a battle of attrition.

The main focus of this deck is to control the board by using Oath of Druids to cheat powerful creatures into play like Terravore and Exalted Angel. The deck also utilizes Cataclysm and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's resources while maintaining card advantage through Sylvan Library. The deck's win condition is to overwhelm the opponent with efficient threats while keeping their own resources intact. The deck also includes removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Aura of Silence to deal with threats from the opponent.

This deck focuses on utilizing enchantments to create a powerful combo engine, fueled by cards like Enchantress's Presence and Mirri's Guile to draw into more enchantments. The goal is to ramp up mana production with cards like Wild Growth and Serra's Sanctum, while controlling the board with Seal of Cleansing and Parallax Wave. The deck can win by creating a lock with Opalescence and Solitary Confinement, or by overwhelming the opponent with creatures pumped up by Opalescence. Replenish is a key card for recursion, allowing the player to bring back crucial enchantments from the graveyard.

The main focus of this deck is to generate mana quickly through enchantments such as Wild Growth and Serra's Sanctum, while drawing cards with Enchantress effects like Argothian Enchantress. The key strategy is to assemble the combo pieces of Opalescence and Solitary Confinement to create a lock that prevents opponents from attacking while turning all enchantments into creatures to win the game. Additionally, the deck includes control elements like Swords to Plowshares and Seal of Cleansing to disrupt opponents' game plan.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by countering opponent's spells, generating card advantage with draw and selection spells, and using land destruction to disrupt the opponent's mana base. The win condition is through efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Wrath of God, with the option of finishing the game with creatures like Exalted Angel or Mishra's Factory. The deck aims to outlast the opponent by maintaining card advantage and board control until it can find an opportunity to close out the game.

The main concept of this deck revolves around utilizing the Replenish combo with cards like Opalescence and Parallax Wave to gain a strong advantage over the opponent. By efficiently cycling through the deck with cards like Attunement and Frantic Search, the goal is to set up a board state that overwhelms the opponent and ultimately leads to victory. The deck also includes control elements like Swords to Plowshares and Decree of Silence to disrupt the opponent's strategy and pave the way for the combo to take over the game.

The deck focuses on using cards like Attunement, Parallax Wave, and Opalescence to quickly assemble a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. Replenish is a key card that allows the player to return multiple enchantments from the graveyard to the battlefield, creating a sudden and devastating advantage. The deck also includes counterspells like Mana Leak and disruption like Seal of Cleansing to protect its combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The goal is to quickly set up a winning board state and overpower the opponent before they have a chance to respond.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and aggressive Goblin creatures. By using cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Matron to put powerful Goblins onto the battlefield quickly, combined with support from cards like Goblin Warchief and Siege-Gang Commander, the deck aims to deal damage swiftly and efficiently. With additional disruption from cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port, the deck is able to control the opponent's resources while maintaining a strong offensive presence on the board.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought with Stifle to create a powerful threat that can be difficult for opponents to deal with. The deck includes various card draw spells and counterspells to help dig for the combo pieces and protect the combo once it is assembled. The inclusion of cards like Opt, Gush, and Portent help ensure the deck can find the key pieces efficiently, while cards like Daze and Counterspell help protect the combo from removal or disruption.

The deck focuses on aggressive land destruction with cards like Armageddon and Wasteland, while also utilizing creatures like Terravore and Nimble Mongoose to quickly close out the game. The deck includes tools to protect against opposing threats, such as Swords to Plowshares and Seal of Cleansing, and has card advantage engines like Sylvan Library to maintain momentum. The strategy is to disrupt the opponent's mana base, establish a dominant board presence, and overwhelm them before they can recover.

The main idea of the deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle, using low-cost creatures like Goblin Cadets, Jackal Pup, and Mogg Fanatic, combined with burn spells such as Lightning Bolt, Incinerate, and Fireblast. The deck focuses on dealing consistent damage to the opponent while also controlling the board with cards like Grim Lavamancer and Cursed Scroll. The mana base is optimized for speed and consistency, with fetch lands like Bloodstained Mire and Wooded Foothills to ensure the right colors of mana are available.

The main focus of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's strategy while controlling the board with removal spells like Smother and Pernicious Deed. The Rock archetype aims to outvalue the opponent by generating card advantage with cards like Genesis and Krosan Tusker. The deck also features a strong presence on the battlefield with creatures like Treetop Village and Blastoderm. Overall, the deck aims to grind out the opponent's resources while slowly building up its own advantage to secure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by using counterspells, removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Wrath of God, and card advantage through cards like Fact or Fiction and Accumulated Knowledge. The deck aims to stabilize by utilizing land-based removal or creature removal, while generating card advantage through draw spells. The win condition is typically through controlling the board until the player can either beat down with creatures like Exalted Angel or create an army with Decree of Justice. The deck also includes disruption tools like Disenchant and Powder Keg to handle troublesome permanents.

The core idea of the deck is to efficiently play and protect Phyrexian Dreadnought while using cards like Stifle and Foil to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Gush and Flash of Insight help find key pieces, while Counterspell and Misdirection provide additional protection. Vision Charm and Portent allow for manipulation of the top of the deck. The deck aims to quickly assemble the combo pieces and control the game until it can emerge victorious.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly combo out Phyrexian Dreadnought by utilizing cards like Stifle, Counterspell, and Foil to protect it from removal. The deck also includes card draw and manipulation spells like Impulse, Gush, and Opt to help find the combo pieces quickly. Additionally, there are control elements like Portent and Thwart to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck aims to assemble the combo as fast as possible while disrupting the opponent's strategy to secure a win.

The goal of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful threat that can win the game in one turn. By using counterspells and card draw spells to protect and find the combo pieces, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while setting up its own win condition. With a focus on efficient card selection and countermagic, the deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and secure victory through a well-timed combo play.

The main concept of this deck is to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan using land destruction and hate cards like Sphere of Resistance. The deck aims to ramp up with Mox Diamond and generate card advantage with Sylvan Library, while also having the option to use Oath of Druids to cheat out powerful creatures like Terravore. The deck seeks to outvalue and outlast the opponent with efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Winter's Grasp, while utilizing Mishra's Factory and Treetop Village as resilient threats.
